title: [Bug]: Passing `--platform=xxx` causes Podman to pull image
body:
### Issue Description

Passing `--platform=linux/amd64` on linux/amd64 machine makes `podman run` significantly slower.

See also #12682 & #14197.

See the outputs below:

```sh
# First run to warm up the local cache
$ podman run --rm --platform=linux/amd64 ghcr.io/reddit/thrift-compiler:0.17.0 --version
<output omitted>
$ time podman run --rm --platform=linux/amd64 ghcr.io/reddit/thrift-compiler:0.17.0 --version
Thrift version 0.17.0

real    0m1.760s
user    0m0.229s
sys     0m0.045s

# First run to warm up the local cache
$ podman run --rm ghcr.io/reddit/thrift-compiler:0.17.0 --version
<output omitted>
$ time podman run --rm ghcr.io/reddit/thrift-compiler:0.17.0 --version
Thrift version 0.17.0

real    0m0.310s
user    0m0.067s
sys     0m0.038s
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. run `podman run --rm --platform=linux/amd64 ghcr.io/reddit/thrift-compiler:0.17.0 --version`
2. also run `podman run --rm ghcr.io/reddit/thrift-compiler:0.17.0 --version`
3. compare the time it took for the above two runs


### Describe the results you received

The one with `--platform=linux/amd64` is significantly slower then the one without.

### Describe the results you expected

They should take roughly the same time to run.

### podman info output

### Privileged Or Rootless

comments.body: Thanks for filing.  Podman will pull an image if the user specified a custom platform (see #10682).  I will see whether we can optimize something.
Opened https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1287 to fix/optimize for this specific case where the platforms match.
Closing since https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1287 got merged.  The fix will make it into the upcoming Podman 4.4 release.
Note that it turned out having caused issues (see https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1299), so we had to revert the change.
should we reopen  this issue then?

Yes, good idea.

So at the moment this is "expected behavior".  But I am sure we can improve on that.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Hello! This is Real Big Problem when the image is *only* local. How can I help move this forward?

We have the same issue here.  No problem when the image is only local however, but really annoying when trying to work locally on updating a Dockerfile, when podman decides to pull the old official version just because `--platform` was used.

What's the rationale behind making `--platform` special?  AFAICT, when the local image has been built with a non-default `--platform` and `podman run` does not pass `--platform`, it makes little sense to try using that local image, in much the same way than the situation when `--platform` is passed to `podman run` only, right?

Wouldn't it be more correct to check if the image platform matches the requested platform, whatever the means used to set the platform (ie. `--platform` or system default)?
